title: A Stranger in the Cold
description: You pass by a shivering figure huddled in a doorway. Their eyes meet yours—tired, hopeful, resigned. The winter wind bites at both of you.

choices.description: Give them your coat and buy them a warm meal.
choices.logMessage: Someone was in need on the street and you opened your heart to them.

choices.description: Avert your eyes and walk past quickly.
choices.logMessage: You passed by someone in need without stopping.

choices.description: Call a shelter and wait with them until help arrives.
choices.logMessage: Someone was in need on the street; you found them help and stayed until it arrived.

title: The Charity Drive
description: Your community is organizing a charity drive for families in need. Flyers are everywhere, and volunteers are signing up at the town hall.

choices.description: Lead the drive and recruit others to help.
choices.logMessage: Your community rallied for a cause and you led the effort.

choices.description: Donate money anonymously and move on.
choices.logMessage: You gave to those in need without seeking recognition.

choices.description: Ignore it—you have your own problems.
choices.logMessage: A chance to help your community came and you turned away, caught up in your own troubles.

title: The Injured Creature
description: You find a small animal—a bird or a cat—lying injured by the roadside. It's still breathing but clearly in pain. No one else is around.

choices.description: Carefully take it to a vet, paying for treatment.
choices.logMessage: An injured creature needed care; you took it to a vet and paid for its treatment.

choices.description: Try to help it yourself with what you have.
choices.logMessage: An injured creature needed care; you did what you could with what you had.

choices.description: Leave it—nature will take its course.
choices.logMessage: An injured creature lay before you; you left it to fate.

title: Violence in the Alley
description: You round a corner and see two people—one is clearly the aggressor, the other is backed against a wall. You're the only witness.

choices.description: Intervene and try to stop the fight.
choices.logMessage: You witnessed violence and chose to step in and try to stop it.

choices.description: Call the authorities and stay to give a statement.
choices.logMessage: You witnessed violence; you called for help and stayed to give a statement.

choices.description: Walk away—it's not your business.
choices.logMessage: You witnessed violence and walked away without getting involved.

title: The Stage Awaits
description: You've been asked to give a speech in front of a large audience. The room will be packed. Your palms are already sweating.

choices.description: Accept and prepare thoroughly—you'll face the fear.
choices.logMessage: You were asked to speak in front of a crowd; you faced your fear and prepared thoroughly.

choices.description: Accept but wing it—confidence is key.
choices.logMessage: You were asked to speak in front of a crowd; you said yes and chose to wing it.

choices.description: Decline—you're not ready for that.
choices.logMessage: You were asked to speak in front of a crowd and decided you were not ready.

title: The Bully
description: Someone has been tormenting a classmate for weeks. Today they've cornered their victim again. Everyone is watching to see what happens.

choices.description: Confront the bully directly and defend the victim.
choices.logMessage: Someone was being bullied and you stood up for them.

choices.description: Get a teacher—let authority handle it.
choices.logMessage: Someone was being bullied; you brought in a teacher to handle it.

choices.description: Look away—you don't want to get involved.
choices.logMessage: Someone was being bullied and you looked away.

title: The Night Before
description: Your friends are throwing a party tonight. Your most important exam is tomorrow morning. The invitation is burning a hole in your pocket.

choices.description: Stay home and study—the exam matters more.
choices.logMessage: The night before a big exam, you chose to stay in and study.

choices.description: Go for an hour, then come back to study.
choices.logMessage: The night before a big exam, you went out for a short while then came back to study.

choices.description: Forget the exam—you'll wing it. Party all night.
choices.logMessage: The night before a big exam, you chose to party and take your chances.

title: The Easy Way Out
description: A colleague suggests cutting corners on a project to meet the deadline. No one would notice, they say. The proper way would take twice as long.

choices.description: Refuse—do the job properly, even if it's late.
choices.logMessage: A colleague suggested cutting corners; you did the job properly instead.

choices.description: Take the shortcut—everyone does it sometimes.
choices.logMessage: You were tempted to cut corners at work and took the shortcut.

choices.description: Report the suggestion to your supervisor.
choices.logMessage: A colleague suggested cutting corners; you reported it to your supervisor.

title: A Questionable Opportunity
description: Someone offers you a deal that would solve your money problems overnight. It's technically legal but morally gray. They need an answer soon.

choices.description: Decline—your principles aren't for sale.
choices.logMessage: You were offered a morally gray opportunity and turned it down.

choices.description: Take the deal—you need the money.
choices.logMessage: You were offered a morally gray opportunity and took it because you needed the money.

choices.description: Ask for time to think—then seek advice from someone you trust.
choices.logMessage: You were offered a morally gray opportunity; you asked for time and sought advice before deciding.

title: The Gray Area
description: You've discovered something that could hurt people if revealed—but keeping it secret could also cause harm. There's no clear right answer.

choices.description: Reveal the truth and accept the consequences.
choices.logMessage: You discovered a difficult truth and chose to reveal it, accepting the consequences.

choices.description: Keep it secret—sometimes ignorance is kinder.
choices.logMessage: You discovered a difficult truth and chose to keep it secret, believing silence was kinder.

choices.description: Seek counsel from someone wiser before deciding.
choices.logMessage: You discovered a difficult truth and sought the counsel of someone wiser before deciding.

title: A Guiding Hand
description: Someone experienced has taken an interest in you. They offer to teach you what they know—but it will require dedication and humility to learn.

choices.description: Accept gratefully and commit to learning.
choices.logMessage: Someone offered to mentor you and you accepted with an open heart.

choices.description: Thank them but decline—you prefer to learn on your own.
choices.logMessage: Someone offered to mentor you; you thanked them but preferred to find your own way.

choices.description: Accept but only take what's useful—you know your path.
choices.logMessage: Someone offered to mentor you; you accepted but took only what served your path.

title: The Hard Truth
description: Something you believed about yourself or the world has been proven wrong. The realization is uncomfortable but undeniable.

choices.description: Embrace the lesson and grow from it.
choices.logMessage: Something you believed was proven wrong; you embraced the lesson and grew from it.

choices.description: Reject it—you're not ready to change.
choices.logMessage: You were confronted with a hard truth and were not ready to accept it.

choices.description: Share the lesson with others who might benefit.
choices.logMessage: You learned a hard lesson and shared it with others who might benefit.

title: The Open Road
description: A friend suggests dropping everything and taking a spontaneous trip. No plans, no itinerary—just adventure. You have responsibilities, but the idea is intoxicating.

choices.description: Say yes—life is too short for endless planning.
choices.logMessage: A friend suggested a spontaneous trip and you said yes without a second thought.

choices.description: Compromise—plan a shorter, structured trip together.
choices.logMessage: A friend suggested a spontaneous trip; you compromised and planned something shorter together.

choices.description: Decline—you have too much to do.
choices.logMessage: A friend suggested a spontaneous trip and you declined; you had too much to do.

title: Show Your Talent
description: A local contest is accepting entries—art, writing, music, whatever you create. The prize isn't much, but the chance to share your work is real.

choices.description: Enter with something you've been working on.
choices.logMessage: You entered a creative contest with something you had already been working on.

choices.description: Create something new just for this.
choices.logMessage: A creative contest came along and you made something new just for it.

choices.description: Skip it—you're not ready for that kind of exposure.
choices.logMessage: You had a chance to share your work in a contest and decided you were not ready for the exposure.

title: The Surprise
description: Your friends have secretly planned a surprise party for you. You've just walked in. Balloons, cake, and familiar faces everywhere.

choices.description: Embrace it fully—dance, laugh, enjoy every moment.
choices.logMessage: Your friends threw you a surprise party and you threw yourself into the moment.

choices.description: Thank everyone warmly but stay somewhat reserved.
choices.logMessage: Your friends threw you a surprise party; you thanked everyone warmly but stayed a little reserved.

choices.description: Feel overwhelmed and need a moment alone.
choices.logMessage: Your friends threw you a surprise party and you were overwhelmed; you needed a moment alone.

title: In the Spotlight
description: You're being honored at a ceremony. People are applauding. Speeches are being made about your achievements. All eyes are on you.

choices.description: Accept graciously and thank those who helped you.
choices.logMessage: You were honored at a ceremony and accepted graciously, thanking those who had helped you.

choices.description: Bask in it—you earned this.
choices.logMessage: You were honored at a ceremony and basked in the recognition; you felt you had earned it.

choices.description: Deflect credit to your team and mentors.
choices.logMessage: You were honored at a ceremony and deflected the credit to your team and mentors.

title: When Things Fall Apart
description: Something you worked hard for has failed. A project, a relationship, a goal—it's over. The disappointment is heavy.

choices.description: Accept it, learn from it, and move forward.
choices.logMessage: Something you worked hard for failed; you accepted it, learned from it, and moved on.

choices.description: Blame others and refuse to accept responsibility.
choices.logMessage: When things fell apart, you blamed others and refused to take responsibility.

choices.description: Let it crush you for a while—you need to grieve.
choices.logMessage: When things fell apart, you let yourself be crushed for a while; you needed to grieve.

title: Whose Idea Was It?
description: A project you contributed to is getting praise. Your contribution was significant, but so were others'. The boss is asking who led the effort.

choices.description: Share credit equally with the team.
choices.logMessage: When credit was up for grabs on a project, you shared it equally with your team.

choices.description: Claim it was mostly your work.
choices.logMessage: When credit was up for grabs on a project, you claimed it was mostly your work.

choices.description: Deflect to the person who had the original idea.
choices.logMessage: When credit was up for grabs on a project, you deflected it to the person who had the original idea.

title: Running on Empty
description: The year has been relentless. Your body and mind are sending signals—exhaustion, irritability, a sense that you cannot keep this pace.

choices.description: Take a step back and prioritize rest.
choices.logMessage: You felt close to burnout and chose to step back and rest.

choices.description: Push through—you have too much at stake.
choices.logMessage: You felt close to burnout but pushed through despite the cost.

title: A Call from Home
description: Your parent has fallen ill. The doctor says it is serious but manageable with care. Your family is asking how much you can help.

choices.description: Make time to visit and help regularly.
choices.logMessage: When your parent fell ill, you made time to visit and help regularly.

choices.description: Send money but keep your distance.
choices.logMessage: When your parent fell ill, you sent money but kept your distance.

title: The Reorganization
description: Rumors have become reality: the company is restructuring. Your role might change, move, or disappear. Everyone is anxious.

choices.description: Stay calm and wait for the dust to settle.
choices.logMessage: During the company reorganization, you stayed calm and waited to see how things would unfold.

choices.description: Start looking for other opportunities.
choices.logMessage: During the company reorganization, you started looking for other opportunities.

title: The Invitation
description: A friend is throwing a party and wants you there. It is the kind of night that could go anywhere—fun, chaos, or both.

choices.description: Go and enjoy yourself.
choices.logMessage: You were invited to a party and went to enjoy yourself.

choices.description: Decline—you need a quiet night.
choices.logMessage: You were invited to a party but declined in favor of a quiet night.

title: A Chance to Ease the Load
description: Your dedication has not gone unnoticed. A scholarship opportunity has opened up—partial funding in exchange for good standing and a short essay.

choices.description: Apply and give it your best shot.
choices.logMessage: You applied for a scholarship and gave it your best shot.

choices.description: Pass—you do not have the time to apply.
choices.logMessage: A scholarship opportunity came up but you passed; you did not have time to apply.

title: The Big Offer
description: A well-known tech company has reached out. They have seen your work and want to talk. The name alone would open doors for years.

choices.description: Pursue the opportunity seriously.
choices.logMessage: You pursued a big tech opportunity and took the process seriously.

choices.description: Stay where you are—stability matters more.
choices.logMessage: A big tech opportunity came your way but you chose to stay where you were; stability mattered more.

title: The Case Everyone Is Watching
description: A high-profile case has landed on your desk. The media will follow every move. Win or lose, your name will be in the spotlight.

choices.description: Take the case and give it everything.
choices.logMessage: You took on a high-profile case and gave it everything.

choices.description: Pass to a senior colleague.
choices.logMessage: A high-profile case came your way; you passed it to a senior colleague.

title: The Code
description: A patient is crashing. You have minutes to decide the next move. The team is looking at you.

choices.description: Stay calm and follow protocol.
choices.logMessage: When a patient was in crisis, you stayed calm and followed protocol.

choices.description: Take a calculated risk to save them.
choices.logMessage: When a patient was in crisis, you took a calculated risk to save them.

title: An Editor's Interest
description: An editor has read your work and wants to discuss a potential project. It could be the break you have been waiting for.

choices.description: Meet and pitch your best idea.
choices.logMessage: An editor showed interest; you met and pitched your best idea.

choices.description: Politely decline—you are not ready.
choices.logMessage: An editor showed interest but you declined; you felt you were not ready.

title: 48 Hours to Build
description: Your department is putting together a team for a hackathon. It is intense, collaborative, and could lead to real opportunities.

choices.description: Join the team and give it your all.
choices.logMessage: You joined a hackathon team and gave it your all.

choices.description: Skip it—you need to focus on coursework.
choices.logMessage: A hackathon came up but you skipped it to focus on coursework.

title: First Real Responsibility
description: On your clinical rotation, a patient's family is asking you to do something that conflicts with what the attending said. You are caught in the middle.

choices.description: Escalate to the attending and document everything.
choices.logMessage: During your clinical rotation you faced a dilemma; you escalated to the attending and documented everything.

choices.description: Try to satisfy the family without going over the attending's head.
choices.logMessage: During your clinical rotation you faced a dilemma; you tried to satisfy the family without going over the attending's head.

title: The Near Miss
description: During a site visit, you notice something that does not look right. Fixing it would delay the project and cost money. Ignoring it might be fine—or not.

choices.description: Report it and insist on a proper fix.
choices.logMessage: You noticed a safety concern on site and reported it and insisted on a proper fix.

choices.description: Mention it informally and hope it gets addressed.
choices.logMessage: You noticed a safety concern on site and mentioned it informally.
